perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***]
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A and Schedule A-1 attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products", each individual unit of Product "Unit"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***], except that, notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, [***].
perturbation.explanation: By defining "Unit" as an individual unit of "Product", it implies that only items on Schedules A and A-1 can be considered "Products". However, later sections refer to "Tetra Units", creating an in-text contradiction whether items that are not defined in the Schedule A, are to be considered products as well or not. This change introduces ambiguity regarding the definition of Products that Heritage must produce
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2(c) of the Agreement is amended so that the term "Units" as defined therein is now referred to as "Tetra Units".
perturbation.justification: The modified text defines "Unit" as an individual unit of "Product", implying that only items on Schedules A and A-1 can be considered "Products". The contradicted text amends Section 2(c) of the Agreement so that the term "Units" as defined therein is now referred to as "Tetra Units". This creates an in-text contradiction, as the modified text's definition of "Unit" conflicts with the term "Tetra Units", leading to ambiguity regarding which terms should be enforced. The perturbation type matches the definition for "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in Schedule C to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on Schedule C.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l. Heritage shall not, however, purchase ingredients or packaging materials in excess of those required [***].
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces the phrase "relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l" which implies that either schedule may contain the necessary information, making it ambiguous which schedule dictates the ingredients and packaging materials Heritage must purchase. This creates an in-text contradiction, especially considering that the original agreement only mentions "Schedule C" in that section. Also, Adding "Heritage shall not, however, purchase ingredients or packaging materials in excess of those required [***]." introduces ambiguity without specifying required for *what*. In addition it contradicts with following sections, which defines who is in charge of those excess materials
perturbation.location: 4. Section 3 DELIVER, PRICING, BILLING AND PAYMENT a.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 4. Section 3 DELIVER, PRICING, BILLING AND PAYMENT b.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3(c) of the Agreement is amended so that whereas it previously read: "Heritage shall charge Premier [***] as set forth in Schedule C." it now reads: "Heritage shall charge Premier [***] as set forth in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-1."
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by using "relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l", where originally only 'Schedule C' was mentioned. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which is an amended version of the original agreement that is now: "Heritage shall charge Premier [***] as set forth in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-1." Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text is in the same location, the ambiguity in the changed text is also directly inserted into the contradicted text. Also, adding that Heritage cannot exceed the required amount is not specific in *what* amount it cannot exceed, adding to the ambiguity. Therefore, according to the perturbation definition, the Misaligned terminology leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered to Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A, which Schedule may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.changed_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1" creates confusion whether Products must conform to specifications listed on both schedules, or either one suffices. This is especially problematic as the requirements could be completely different, introducing contradiction and uncertainty. Also, the original text defined, what should conform to the specification, which is manufactured, packaged and delivered *to* heritage. In the modified version, it should conform to manufactured, packaged and delivered *by* heritage or jasper, introducing uncertainty regarding the definition of what *manufactured, packaged and delivered* refers to
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2(a) of the Agreement is amended so that the first sentence that previously read: "Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***]" Source: BELLRING BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/20/2019 now reads: "Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A and Schedule A-1 attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products," each individual unit of Product "Unit"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***], except that, notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, [***]."
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that products should conform to specifications on 'Schedule A'. The changed text introduces 'Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1', creating ambiguity. This ambiguity directly contradicts the amended Section 2(a), which now explicitly references both Schedule A and Schedule A-1. The use of 'and/or' in the changed text makes it unclear whether conformance to both schedules is required or if fulfilling either schedule suffices, which contradicts the explicit mention of both schedules in the amended Section 2(a). Also, the explanation given, states that original text specified manufacture, package and delivery *to* heritage, and the changed text defines manufacture, package and delivery *by* heritage, which creates further uncertainty.
